title: The Chimney Sweeper by William Blake | Line By Line Explanation in Hindi
description:
Few poems expose the machinery of childhood exploitation as quietly and devastatingly as William Blake's The Chimney Sweeper — a deceptively simple lyric from Songs of Innocence where a child's suffering is dressed in the language of hope, making the reader complicit in the very comfort it offers. Blake wrote this at a time when thousands of boys were sold into chimney sweeping, and the poem's tension between innocence and systemic cruelty makes it essential reading for BA English Honours students at DU, SOL, and IGNOU. If you've ever wondered how a nursery-rhyme tone can carry the weight of social protest, this explanation will change how you read Blake forever.
